questions.stem: For unity feedback closed loop system with G(s) = K(s+1)/[s(s+5)(s+20)(s+50)], K=2500: Draw the asymptotic Bode plot.
common_errors: Missing zero at s=-1 contribution; wrong slope transitions
Not accounting for the zero when computing initial slope (-20 dB/dec from integrator)
questions.solution_method: Express G(s) in Bode standard form: G(s) = (2500/5000)*(s+1)/[s*(1+s/5)*(1+s/20)*(1+s/50)]. DC gain = 0.5 = -6 dB. Plot asymptotes: start at -6 dB with slope -20 dB/dec (integrator), slope changes at corner frequencies ω=1 (zero, +20), ω=5 (pole, -20), ω=20 (pole, -20), ω=50 (pole, -20 dB/dec).
key_formulas: Slope contribution: zero +20 dB/dec, pole -20 dB/dec

questions.stem: For G(s) = 2500(s+1)/[s(s+5)(s+20)(s+50)], find the gain margin (GM) and phase margin (PM) from the Bode plot.
common_errors: Confusing phase crossover frequency with gain crossover frequency
Sign error when computing GM in dB (GM = -|G(jω_pc)|dB)
Not accounting for the zero's phase contribution (+arctan(ω))
questions.solution_method: Find phase crossover frequency ω_pc where ∠G(jω)=-180°; compute GM = -20log10|G(jω_pc)|. Find gain crossover frequency ω_gc where |G(jω)|=0 dB; compute PM = 180° + ∠G(jω_gc). From the Bode diagram shown: GM = 31.2 dB at ω=35.8 rad/s, PM = 111° at ω=0.572 rad/s.

questions.stem: For G(s) = 2500(s+1)/[s(s+5)(s+20)(s+50)], determine the stability of the system.
common_errors: Not checking both GM and PM before concluding stability
questions.solution_method: System is stable if GM > 0 dB and PM > 0°. With GM = 31.2 dB and PM = 111°, both are positive, so the closed-loop system is stable.
key_formulas: Stable if GM > 0 dB and PM > 0°

questions.stem: For G(s) = 2500(s+1)/[s(s+5)(s+20)(s+50)], what is the effect on GM and PM if K increases 10 times (K becomes 25000)?
common_errors: Thinking PM changes by same amount as GM
Forgetting that phase crossover frequency doesn't shift with gain change
Not recognising that gain crossover frequency shifts right, changing PM
questions.solution_method: Increasing K by factor 10 shifts the Bode magnitude plot up by 20 dB uniformly; phase plot is unaffected. GM decreases by 20 dB (from 31.2 dB to ~11.2 dB). Gain crossover frequency shifts to higher ω where phase is more negative, so PM also decreases. System may still be stable but with reduced margins.
key_formulas: Phase plot unchanged with gain variation

questions.stem: Determine the transfer function of a minimum phase system whose Bode magnitude plot is obtained experimentally. The plot shows: initial slope -40 dB/dec, magnitude ~60 dB at ω=0.1 rad/s, a break at ω=1 rad/s where slope changes to -20 dB/dec, and the magnitude is ~20 dB at ω=1 rad/s.
common_errors: Misidentifying initial slope: -40 dB/dec means double integrator (1/s^2), not single
Wrong DC gain extraction — must extrapolate initial asymptote to ω=1
Not checking minimum phase assumption for phase consistency
questions.solution_method: Initial slope -40 dB/dec → two poles at origin (1/s²). Break at ω=1 where slope changes to -20 dB/dec → one zero at s=-1 (factor s+1). At ω=1, magnitude asymptote reads 20 dB → |K/1²|=10, so K=10. TF = 10(s+1)/s².
key_formulas: Slope -40 dB/dec → n poles at origin, n=2
Slope change +20 dB/dec at corner ω_c → zero at s = -ω_c
Magnitude at ω=1: 20 log10(K) = 20 dB → K = 10

notes: Tutorial dated 7 April 2026. Two questions; Q1 has four sub-parts (A-D) all on the same G(s). Bode diagram with GM=31.2 dB at 35.8 rad/s, PM=111° at 0.572 rad/s is provided as indicative solution visual for Q1. Indicative solution for Q2 given as G(s)=10(s+1)/s².
